result.summary: ComplyAdvantage exposes three documented HTTP integration surfaces: the core ComplyAdvantage REST API, the Mesh API, and a Transaction Monitoring and Screening API; no public GraphQL, MCP server, or CLI was found.
result.description: ComplyAdvantage provides financial crime risk and compliance products, including screening, ongoing monitoring, transaction monitoring, and case management. Its products are offered through its core platform and the ComplyAdvantage Mesh platform.

ca_api_key.label: ComplyAdvantage API key
ca_api_key.setup: Generate an API key within the ComplyAdvantage web platform, as described in the [Authentication section](https://docs.complyadvantage.com/#authentication). The docs state keys are generated in the web platform and then sent on every request as `Authorization: Token YOUR_API_KEY`.

mesh_api_user.label: Mesh API user credentials
mesh_api_user.setup: During onboarding, ComplyAdvantage provides an API user welcome email with instructions to set a password and your organization `realm`. Use the API user's email address, chosen password, and organization name to call the [Create an access token](https://docs.mesh.complyadvantage.com/reference/createtoken) endpoint, as explained in [Getting Started](https://docs.mesh.complyadvantage.com/docs/getting-started).

mesh_access_token.label: Mesh access token
mesh_access_token.setup: First obtain your API user's onboarding credentials, then call [Create an access token](https://docs.mesh.complyadvantage.com/reference/createtoken) with `username`, `password`, and `realm`. Use the returned token as `Authorization: Bearer {access_token}`. The [OpenAPI schema](https://docs.mesh.complyadvantage.com/openapi/mesh-api.json) shows tokens expire after `86400` seconds.

surfaces.name: ComplyAdvantage Transaction Monitoring and Screening API
